scores.metric: full-build unmasked Levenshtein vs pristine code6cac.o (rules NEUTRALIZED); sandbox --disable all reads 13 but masking is unreliable here

sessions.lever: fixed-write base[index] fold + keep inner full-const-addr writes (hybrid)

reviewer.evidence: HEAD form carries 6 regfix subst/delete rules + asm volatile barriers (val coercion, _frame dead-frame). All are cheat-asm/cheat-regfix per inline-asm-policy expanded catalog and the ARCHIVED scratchpad-gte rule. No committable form found.

remaining_gap.summary: Two irreducible non-pure-C gaps after the inner loop is made to match: (1) the two FIXED scratchpad writes 0x1F8000B8 / 0x1F800060 emit lui+ori+sw 0(reg) (3 insns) where target folds to lui+sw offset(at) (2 insns) — GCC 2.7.2 does not reliably fold a 32-bit constant store address into the sw displacement; the 6 regfix subst/delete rules manufacture the fold. (2) target reserves an UNUSED 8-byte stack frame (addiu sp,-8 / +8 with nothing stored) — GCC emits no frame for this body; HEAD forces it with `s32 _frame; asm volatile("":: "m"(_frame))` which is a dead-var+barrier cheat.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: the existing 6 regfix rules (subst $3->$1, fold 0($3)->0xb8($1), delete the ori, two more subst for the 0x60/0xac writes) plus the asm barriers — exactly the recipe the ARCHIVED scratchpad-gte rule forbids.

next_hypotheses: CANONICAL-ASM authorization (user decision): the unused frame + the constant-address store fold are both hallmarks the original was hand-written asm / a different cc1 fold behavior. This is the documented endpoint per scratchpad-gte.md path (b).

If ever revisited in pure C: would need a GCC 2.7.2 idiom that folds a constant 32-bit store address into lui+sw offset — none found across this and the cpu_check_tubazeri family; archived rule says no pure-C path.

rejected_forms.form: idx_base: all scratchpad writes via ((volatile s32*)0x1F800000)[idx], early `return`
rejected_forms.reason: LICM-hoists the inner-loop lui 0x1f80 out of the loop (target re-materializes it each iteration); also drops the frame. Worse than hybrid.

rejected_forms.form: HEAD committed form (6 regfix rules + asm barriers)
rejected_forms.reason: byte-matches but ONLY via cheat-regfix (scratchpad fold subst/delete) + cheat-asm (_frame dead-frame barrier, val coercion barrier). inline-asm-policy + scratchpad-gte ARCHIVED forbid it.
